notes: Form metadata used by generate.mjs renderFormCovers(). Edit titles, manualSection, owner here; do not edit cover PDFs directly.

sections.html: Document each pre-shift toolbox talk. Record attendance for every worker present. Filed with the project safety binder; copy to Safety Director within 48 hours. Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.21 · MISH 04 · MISH 06
Complete a JHA before any new task or critical activity. Walk the work area, identify each step, list the hazards, and document the controls. References: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.21 · ANSI/AIHA Z10 · MISH 05

sections.title: Required PPE
sections.subtitle: Check all that apply for this task

sections.title: Crew Acknowledgment
sections.refNote: By signing, crew members acknowledge they have reviewed this JHA and understand the hazards and controls.

sections.html: Complete this form within 24 hours of any incident, accident, near miss, or property damage. WA L&I Report of Accident (ROA Form F242-130-000) must also be filed for any work-related injury. References: OSHA 29 CFR 1904 · WAC 296-155-110 · RCW 51

sections.title: Signatures & Authorization
sections.refNote: DocuSign Note: Each signature field below corresponds to a DocuSign recipient role. Assign roles before sending. Original signatures, when printed, must be retained with the incident file per WAC 296-27-031.

sections.html: Report any near miss (an unplanned event that did not result in injury or damage but had the potential to do so) within 24 hours. Near-miss reporting is non-disciplinary and is critical to identifying hazards before they cause harm. References: OSHA 29 CFR 1904 · ANSI Z10 · WAC 296-155-110

sections.title: What Happened
sections.subtitle: Describe the event, what could have happened, and why it didn't
sections.name: description

sections.title: Immediate Action Taken
sections.subtitle: Steps taken at the time to remove the hazard
sections.name: immediateAction

sections.title: Signatures & Authorization
sections.refNote: Near-miss reports are reviewed by the Safety Director within 5 business days. Trends are shared anonymously in the next monthly safety meeting per WAC 296-27-031.

sections.html: Complete a documented site safety walk each work day. Mark each item as Pass / Fail / N/A. Log every Fail in the Corrective Actions table with assignment and target date. Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.20(b)(2) · MISH 09

Required onboarding acknowledgment for every worker before performing work on an MH Construction site. Retain in the project safety binder; copy to Safety Director within 24 hours. Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.21(b)(2) · MISH 04

Cover sheet for the annual OSHA Form 300 / 300A / 301 packet. Posted from February 1 – April 30 in a conspicuous location at each establishment per OSHA recordkeeping rule. Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1904 · MISH 17

sections.title: Certification
sections.refNote: By signing, the company executive certifies that the information is true, accurate, and complete (29 CFR 1904.32).

sections.html: Cover sheet for the Washington L&I Report of Accident (ROA) packet supplied to the worker, treating provider, and L&I claim file. Reference: RCW 51.28 · WAC 296-14 · MISH 17

Per-employee training transcript. Records must be retained for the duration of employment plus 3 years (or longer where regulation specifies). Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 / 1926 training subparts · MISH 04 · MISH 06

sections.title: Verification
sections.refNote: Employee signature acknowledges accuracy of training history.

sections.html: Complete this Pre-Task Safety Plan (PTSP / JHA) before starting work on any task with new or changed hazards. All crew members must review and sign before lifting a tool. References: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart C · WAC 296-155-100 · ANSI/ASSP Z590.3

sections.title: Required PPE
sections.subtitle: Check all that apply for this task

sections.title: Permits / Special Procedures Required
sections.subtitle: List any hot-work, confined-space, excavation, or LOTO permits in effect
sections.name: permits

sections.title: Crew Acknowledgment
sections.subtitle: All crew members acknowledge they have reviewed this PTSP and understand the hazards and controls.
sections.name: crew

sections.title: Plan Approval
sections.refNote: This PTSP must be re-evaluated whenever conditions, scope, or crew changes. Retain with the daily site safety inspection per WAC 296-155-110.

sections.html: Required pre-employment consent and authorization for substance testing. Test results are protected health information and shared only with HR and the MRO. Reference: 49 CFR Part 40 (where DOT-applicable) · WAC 296-823 · MISH 06

sections.title: Disclosure of Current Medications
sections.subtitle: List prescription / OTC medications taken in the last 30 days
sections.name: medications

sections.title: Consent & Authorization
sections.refNote: By signing, applicant consents to substance testing as a condition of employment and authorizes release of results to MH Construction HR.

sections.html: Authorization for substance testing following a recordable workplace accident or near-miss. Sample collection should occur within 2 hours of the incident (8 hours max for drug, 32 hours for alcohol). Reference: 49 CFR Part 40 · OSHA 29 CFR 1904.35(b)(1)(iv) · MISH 06 · MISH 17

sections.title: Authorization
sections.refNote: By signing, the employee consents to post-accident testing per the MH Construction substance abuse policy. Refusal is grounds for termination per policy.

sections.html: Documentation by trained supervisor of specific, articulable physical and behavioral observations supporting reasonable suspicion. Required before referring an employee for for-cause testing. Reference: 49 CFR § 382.307 / Part 40 · MISH 06

sections.title: Specific Articulable Facts
sections.subtitle: Describe what you saw, heard, smelled — facts only
sections.name: facts

sections.title: Verification
sections.refNote: Two trained supervisors should concur where practical.

sections.html: Authorization for MH Construction to obtain Motor Vehicle Records and add the employee to the company driver list. MVR review repeats annually. Reference: FCRA 15 U.S.C. §1681 · WA RCW 46.52 · MISH 18

sections.title: Authorization & Disclosure
sections.refNote: Driver authorizes MH Construction (and its agents) to obtain MVRs annually and acknowledges company driving policies.

sections.html: Pre-trip inspection required before each shift for company vehicles and CDL-class trucks. Tag out and report any safety-critical defect to the Fleet Manager. Reference: 49 CFR § 392.7 / 396.11 (CDL) · MISH 18

Complete this report within 24 hours of any motor vehicle accident involving an MH Construction vehicle, rented vehicle, or personal vehicle being used for company business. Notify Travelers Insurance and law enforcement as appropriate. References: WA RCW 46.52 · FMCSA 49 CFR 390 · WAC 296-155

sections.title: Description of Accident
sections.subtitle: Who, what, when, where, how — driver's narrative
sections.name: description

sections.title: Post-Accident Testing
sections.subtitle: Required per FORM 03-C — check all that apply

sections.title: Damage Description
sections.subtitle: Describe damage to MH vehicle, other vehicles, and property
sections.name: damage

sections.title: Signatures & Authorization
sections.refNote: Attach photos, police report, and other-party insurance card to this form. Forward immediately to the Safety Director per WAC 296-27-031.

sections.html: Pre-shift inspection required before each operating period. Tag out and remove from service any equipment with safety-critical defects. Reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.601 / 1926.602 · ANSI/SAE · MISH 19 · MISH 20 · MISH 42

sections.title: Defects / Items Needing Attention
sections.subtitle: Tag out equipment with safety-critical defects
sections.name: defects

sections.html: Required vetting form for any subcontractor performing work on an MH Construction project. Submit to MH Procurement before contract award. References: AGC CSEA · ISN/Avetta equivalents · MISH 46

sections.title: Safety Program Confirmation
sections.subtitle: Provide written program for each item checked

sections.title: Authorized Officer Certification
sections.refNote: By signing, the authorized officer certifies the information is true, accurate, and complete.
